what_it_does: Filesystem-backed addressed store implementing the Resolver contract. It stores content-addressed immutable JSON and binary objects, mutable refs with version history, provenance records with lineage walking, a memo gate, graphs, review sessions, scale pyramids, journeys, agent-session registry and timeline records, chat-thread metadata, append-only logs for events, chat turns, annotations, coherence findings, marks, pins, dispositions, agent mail, and surfaced decisions, plus a cached vector index with per-space matrix warming. All filesystem-specific locking and fsync durability is kept inside this module so callers are backend-agnostic.
observations: Uses a custom _CrossProcessLock that composes a threading.RLock with fcntl.flock and a per-thread nesting-depth counter to make cross-process locks re-entrant.
All durable pointer writes are funneled through the module-level _atomic_write_fsync (tmp + fsync + os.replace + parent-dir fsync).
CAS and blob objects are write-once under objects/; mutable refs live separately under refs/ and have an append-only ref_history/ trail.
Vector indexing uses an in-memory cache keyed by per-file (mtime_ns, size) signatures and a per-(space, emb) numpy matrix cache keyed by a version bump.
Layers self-description is memoized and gated by a single stat of the vectors/ directory mtime.
append_event uses an in-process RLock to keep seq monotonic within one process but explicitly notes it is not cross-process-unique.
The file contains extensive inline design rationale for nearly every method, citing durability, race conditions, and backend portability.

Many distinct subdirectories and append-only jsonl files are created: objects, refs, ref_history, meta, memo, graphs, sessions, scale, journeys, agent_sessions, chat_threads, surfaced, locks, vectors, plus events.jsonl, chat.jsonl, annotations.jsonl, findings.jsonl, etc.
standouts: Cross-process re-entrant locking via RLock + fcntl.flock with per-thread depth tracking.
Centralized atomic durable write primitive used by almost every mutable store path.
Resolver-seam portability: all fsync/locking/fsync details are hidden inside FsStore.
Vector cache with mtime/size revalidation and optional per-space cosine matrix warm threshold.
conventions: Atomic writes use a unique temp per pid/thread, fsync the temp, os.replace, then fsync the parent directory.
Append-only jsonl logs record history for refs, events, chat, annotations, findings, marks, agent mail, and pins.
Fail-loud guards when a CAS/blob path is a directory or when an agent-session record lacks an id.
In-process RLock for hot store-level paths; cross-process lock for whole-graph and dispatch-claim mutations.
Open/additive record schemas that splat caller fields plus a timestamp.
Content-addressed immutable objects are separate from mutable pointers and overlays.
Local imports inside methods to keep top-level dependencies minimal.
concerns: append_event only serializes seq within one process; the docstring admits two processes can still emit duplicate seq values.
Several jsonl read paths load the entire file into memory (read_text + splitlines), which may become expensive as logs grow.
The _safe filename encoder is a simple string replacement and could theoretically collide for distinct addresses.
The vector cache refresh holds a lock while scanning and parsing changed files, which may stall concurrent queries.
The provided file is truncated, so the full implementation of vector, surfaced, mail, disposition, mark, and pin methods is not visible.
notes: This is the filesystem substrate every face of the system writes through: it owns the crash-durable, cross-process locking and atomic-write primitives that keep the addressed store backend-agnostic.
questions: Where is the contracts.resolver.Resolver interface that FsStore claims to implement defined?
What callers populate and consume vector records, and what is the exact record schema?
What are the consumers and schemas for surfaced decisions, agent mail, marks, pins, and dispositions?
Is there an acceptance test that verifies the concurrent-storm guarantees mentioned for ref_history and event seq?
How would a Supabase backend counterpart implement graph_lock/dispatch_lock with advisory locks while keeping the same API?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is a filesystem-backed addressed store with content-addressed objects, mutable refs, provenance, lineage, memo, and locking; the file implements that and extends it to sessions, journeys, agent sessions, chat threads, vectors, surfaced state, and mail.
apparent_themes: Persistence as a dumb substrate rather than a smart service.
Crash durability and cross-process concurrency hidden behind a backend seam.
Inline documentation as a design record.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be the single backend-agnostic filesystem implementation of the store contract, demonstrating that durability (fsync) and cross-process serialization (flock) can be encapsulated so the rest of the engine stays portable to a hosted backend.
novelty: The explicit composition of re-entrant thread locking with per-thread fcntl depth tracking, and the central module-level atomic-write primitive that every mutable store path funnels through, are relatively unusual for a small filesystem-backed store.
contribution: It contributes a complete, self-contained filesystem resolver with atomic durable writes, cross-process re-entrant locks, content-addressed immutable objects, append-only history logs, and in-memory vector-index caching — all behind a backend-agnostic API that a fused system can keep as the local store backend or replace without touching callers.
summary_for_embedding: Filesystem-backed addressed store (FsStore) providing content-addressed immutable objects, mutable refs with version history, provenance/lineage, memoization, graph/session/journey/agent-session/chat-thread persistence, append-only event/annotation/finding/mail logs, and cached vector indexing with cross-process locking and crash-durable atomic writes.
intention_for_embedding: To encapsulate all filesystem-specific concurrency and durability primitives behind a single resolver seam so the engine can treat local disk and a future hosted backend identically.

symbols.does: Re-entrant cross-process lock class.
symbols.description: Composes a threading.RLock for in-process re-entrancy with an fcntl.flock on a per-key lockfile for cross-process serialization. A per-thread depth counter ensures the OS file-lock is taken only on the outermost acquire and released only on the outermost exit, making nested calls safe.

symbols.does: Initializes the cross-process lock state.
symbols.description: Stores the lockfile path, creates a thread RLock, and initializes per-thread maps for nesting depth and the open file descriptor that currently holds the flock.

symbols.does: Context manager that acquires and releases the composed lock.
symbols.description: Acquires the thread RLock, increments per-thread depth, opens the lockfile and takes an exclusive flock only on the outermost acquire, yields, then releases the flock and fd only on the outermost exit and finally releases the RLock.

symbols.does: Fsyncs a directory entry for durability.
symbols.description: Opens a directory read-only and calls os.fsync so that a preceding rename or create within it is durable on crash; best-effort on platforms that refuse to fsync a directory fd.

symbols.does: Atomically and durably writes a string to a file.
symbols.description: Writes to a unique temp file named with pid and thread id, fsyncs the temp fd, atomically renames it over the target with os.replace, then fsyncs the parent directory so the rename survives a crash.

symbols.does: Filesystem-backed addressed store class.
symbols.description: Implements the Resolver contract for a local filesystem backend, exposing content-addressed objects, mutable refs, provenance, lineage, memo, graphs, sessions, journeys, agent sessions, chat threads, surfaced state, vector indexing, and several append-only logs. It encapsulates all fcntl/fsync concurrency inside the store seam.

symbols.does: Initializes store directories, locks, and caches.
symbols.description: Creates the root and subdirectories for objects, refs, graphs, sessions, vectors, etc.; sets up in-process RLocks for events and surfaced state, a guard for per-graph cross-process locks, and in-memory caches for vectors, per-space matrices, and layer self-description.

symbols.does: Builds a filename-safe lockfile name.
symbols.description: Reuses the address-safe filename encoder and appends .lock so arbitrary keys such as graph ids and dispatch-claim keys become valid lockfile names.

symbols.does: Returns a re-entrant cross-process lock for a graph id.
symbols.description: Creates a _CrossProcessLock on demand under locks/ for the given graph id. The whole-graph load-mutate-save paths hold this lock so concurrent mutations, even across separate processes, cannot produce lost updates. The API is a context manager identical to a thread RLock.

symbols.does: Alias for the dispatch-claim cross-process lock.
symbols.description: Routes to graph_lock with a dispatch-claim: prefix key, providing the same cross-process primitive for the exactly-once dispatch check-claim path so there is one locking mechanism with two call spellings.

symbols.does: Hashes bytes into a cas:// address.
symbols.description: Computes a 16-byte blake2b digest and prefixes it with cas://b2: to produce a content-addressed identifier for JSON objects.

symbols.does: Encodes an address into a safe filename.
symbols.description: Replaces :// with __, : with _, and / with _ so addresses such as cas://... and run://... become valid, flat filesystem names.

symbols.does: Stores an immutable JSON object and returns its CAS address.
symbols.description: Serializes data to canonical JSON, hashes it, writes the bytes write-once under objects/, and fails loudly if the target path is a directory. Returns the cas:// address.

symbols.does: Retrieves a JSON object by CAS address.
symbols.description: Reads the bytes from objects/ and deserializes them with json.loads.

symbols.does: Checks whether a CAS object exists.
symbols.description: Returns True if the file for the given cas:// address exists under objects/.

symbols.does: Hashes bytes into a blob:// address.
symbols.description: Computes a 16-byte blake2b digest and prefixes it with blob://b2: to produce a content-addressed identifier for raw binary objects.

symbols.does: Stores raw binary bytes and returns a blob:// address.
symbols.description: Validates that the input is bytes, hashes it, writes the bytes write-once under objects/, and fails loudly if the target path is a directory. Returns the blob:// address.

symbols.does: Retrieves raw bytes by blob:// address.
symbols.description: Reads and returns the raw bytes from objects/ for the given blob:// address.

symbols.does: Checks whether a blob object exists.
symbols.description: Returns True if the file for the given blob:// address exists under objects/.

symbols.does: Method alias to the module-level atomic write primitive.
symbols.description: Routes self._fsync_atomic_write calls to the module-level _atomic_write_fsync function so there is one durable write implementation and both call spellings work.

symbols.does: Atomically sets a mutable ref to a CAS address.
symbols.description: Writes the new CAS address to refs/<logical> using the durable atomic-write primitive so readers always see the whole old or whole new value, never a torn or empty file. After the write succeeds it appends a version-history entry.

symbols.does: Appends a version-history entry for a ref.
symbols.description: Writes a single {ts, cas} jsonl line to ref_history/<logical> using an O_APPEND file handle; the append is lock-free and separate from the live ref write.

symbols.does: Returns the version trail of a ref.
symbols.description: Reads the ref_history jsonl file for the logical address and returns chronological {ts, cas} entries, optionally limited to the newest N while preserving order.

symbols.does: Reads the current value of a mutable ref.
symbols.description: Returns the contents of refs/<logical> as a string, or None if the ref has never been written.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a Provenance record.
symbols.description: Writes the provenance model as JSON to meta/<safe(address)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive so concurrent readers never see torn or empty JSON.

symbols.does: Reads and validates a Provenance record.
symbols.description: If meta/<safe(address)>.json exists, reads it and returns a Provenance pydantic model; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Walks provenance inputs back to sources.
symbols.description: Performs a breadth-first walk over provenance inputs starting from the given address, de-duplicating and cycle-safe, returning the visited addresses in dependency order.

symbols.does: Reads the memo gate value for a signature.
symbols.description: Returns the CAS address stored in memo/<safe(sig)>, or None if no memo entry exists.

symbols.does: Atomically sets the memo gate value.
symbols.description: Writes the CAS address to memo/<safe(sig)> using the durable atomic-write primitive so concurrent memo_get calls never read a torn or empty value.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a graph record.
symbols.description: Writes the graph model as JSON to graphs/<safe(id)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive with a unique temp per pid/thread, so concurrent whole-graph mutations last-writer-wins with whole files.

symbols.does: Reads and validates a graph record.
symbols.description: If graphs/<safe(gid)>.json exists, reads it and returns a Graph pydantic model; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a review-session record.
symbols.description: Writes the session dict as JSON to sessions/<safe(id)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive so concurrent reads on the threading bridge never see a torn file.

symbols.does: Reads a review-session record.
symbols.description: If sessions/<safe(sid)>.json exists, reads and returns it as a dict; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Lists review-session ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in the sessions/ directory, or an empty list if the directory does not exist.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a scale-pyramid structure record.
symbols.description: Writes the pyramid dict as JSON to scale/<safe(key)>.json, where the key is the space or space#emb=<emb>, using the durable atomic-write primitive.

symbols.does: Reads a scale-pyramid structure record.
symbols.description: If scale/<safe(key)>.json exists, reads and returns it as a dict; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Lists scale-pyramid record keys.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in the scale/ directory, or an empty list if the directory does not exist.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a journey record.
symbols.description: Writes the journey dict as JSON to journeys/<safe(id)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive, giving readers whole-old-or-whole-new semantics.

symbols.does: Reads a journey record.
symbols.description: If journeys/<safe(jid)>.json exists, reads and returns it as a dict; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Lists journey ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in the journeys/ directory, or an empty list if the directory does not exist.

symbols.does: Atomically persists an agent-session registry record.
symbols.description: Validates that the record has a non-empty string id, then writes it to agent_sessions/<safe(id)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive; refuses to write an unfindable record.

symbols.does: Reads an agent-session registry record.
symbols.description: If agent_sessions/<safe(sid)>.json exists, reads and returns it as a dict; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Lists agent-session ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in the agent_sessions/ directory, or an empty list if the directory does not exist.

symbols.does: Returns modification times for agent-session records.
symbols.description: Builds a map from session id to record-file mtime so a registry fold can reload only records that have changed, mirroring an events-since delta discipline.

symbols.does: Atomically persists an agent-session timeline cache.
symbols.description: Validates the session id and writes the timeline record to agent_sessions/timeline/<safe(sid)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive.

symbols.does: Reads an agent-session timeline cache.
symbols.description: If agent_sessions/timeline/<safe(sid)>.json exists, reads and returns it as a dict; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Atomically persists chat-thread metadata.
symbols.description: Writes the thread dict as JSON to chat_threads/<safe(id)>.json using the durable atomic-write primitive; actual turns remain in the shared append-only chat.jsonl.

symbols.does: Reads chat-thread metadata.
symbols.description: If chat_threads/<safe(tid)>.json exists, reads and returns it as a dict; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Lists chat-thread ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in the chat_threads/ directory, or an empty list if the directory does not exist.

symbols.does: Returns chat turns belonging to a thread.
symbols.description: Reads the shared append-only chat.jsonl, filters records by thread_id, and returns the oldest-first last limit turns.

symbols.does: Lists graph ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in the graphs/ directory.

symbols.does: Appends an event with a monotonic sequence number.
symbols.description: Under an in-process RLock, reads the last seq from events.jsonl, increments it, writes a {seq, ts, **event} line, flushes and fsyncs the fd, then returns the record. The docstring notes this is not cross-process-unique.

symbols.does: Returns the newest events.
symbols.description: Reads events.jsonl and returns the newest limit records in newest-first order.

symbols.does: Returns events after a sequence number.
symbols.description: Reads events.jsonl and returns all records with seq greater than the given value in oldest-first order, used as an SSE stream cursor.

symbols.does: Appends a chat turn.
symbols.description: Writes a {ts, **turn} line to the append-only chat.jsonl and returns the record.

symbols.does: Returns recent chat turns.
symbols.description: Reads chat.jsonl and returns the oldest-first last limit turns.

symbols.does: Returns chat turns attached to an address.
symbols.description: Filters the shared append-only chat.jsonl by the address field and returns matching records oldest-first, keeping chat history one-source rather than in a parallel store.

symbols.does: Appends an annotation.
symbols.description: Writes a {ts, **rec} line to the append-only annotations.jsonl and returns the record; the address field is the retrieval key.

symbols.does: Returns annotations at an address.
symbols.description: Filters annotations.jsonl by address and returns matching records oldest-first.

symbols.does: Appends a coherence finding.
symbols.description: Writes a {ts, **rec} line to the append-only findings.jsonl and returns the record; re-detection accrues and read-time reconcile deduplicates by (kind, address).

symbols.does: Returns findings at an address.
symbols.description: Filters findings.jsonl by address and returns matching records oldest-first.

symbols.does: Returns every finding record.
symbols.description: Reads findings.jsonl and returns all non-empty records oldest-first, intended for reconcile and burn-down rollups.

symbols.does: Records a disposition on a finding handle.
symbols.description: Records a human decision such as finish/defer/by-design on the (kind, address) finding handle in a last-writer-wins overlay, separate from the append-only finding log so dispositions survive re-detection.

symbols.does: Retrieves the current disposition for a finding handle.
symbols.description: Returns the recorded disposition for the (kind, address) handle, or None if no disposition has been recorded.

symbols.does: Appends a mark record.
symbols.description: Writes a mark record to the append-only marks log and returns it.

symbols.does: Returns marks attached to a target.
symbols.description: Filters the marks log by target and returns matching records.

symbols.does: Returns marks of a given type.
symbols.description: Filters the marks log by mark_type and returns matching records.

symbols.does: Returns every mark record.
symbols.description: Reads the marks log and returns all non-empty records.

symbols.does: Appends a pin-state record.
symbols.description: Records whether a target timestamp at an address is pinned, appending to the pin log so state accrues rather than overwriting.

symbols.does: Returns the effective pin state for an address.
symbols.description: Reads the pin log and returns the effective pin state for the given address, or None/empty if unpinned.

symbols.does: Builds a canonical vector-space address.
symbols.description: Static method that constructs a normalized address string for a vector space, optionally qualified by an embedder name.

symbols.does: Persists a vector record.
symbols.description: Stores a vector record under the vectors/ substrate keyed by address, recording the vector, content_hash, dimension, model, space, source, and embedder, and returns the stored record.

symbols.does: Retrieves a vector record by address.
symbols.description: Returns the vector record for the given address, or None if it does not exist.

symbols.does: Removes a single vector record.
symbols.description: Deletes the vector record for the given address and returns True if it existed and was removed.

symbols.does: Batch-removes vector records.
symbols.description: Deletes vector records for the given addresses and returns the count removed.

symbols.does: Internal cached loader for vector records.
symbols.description: Scans the vectors/ directory, parses each file once, caches it keyed by a (mtime_ns, size) signature, and re-parses only files whose signature changed, returning the list of records.

symbols.does: Pre-builds the vector cache and matrices.
symbols.description: Loads all vector records into the cache and, for spaces above the warm threshold, pre-builds the per-space cosine matrix; returns the number of records cached.

symbols.does: Lists addresses in a vector space.
symbols.description: Returns the list of addresses stored in the optional space, using the shared vector cache.

symbols.does: Returns vector records for a space/embedder.
symbols.description: Returns the vector records for the given space and embedder, optionally using a supplied records list, leveraging the cache for fast repeated queries.

symbols.does: Returns the cached matrix for a space/embedder.
symbols.description: Returns the pre-built numpy matrix and parallel id list for the given space and embedder, building and caching it on first use if needed.

symbols.does: Returns layers grouped by space.
symbols.description: Returns the memoized self-description mapping of layers grouped by vector space, recomputing only when the vectors directory mtime changes.

symbols.does: Internal builder for layer self-description.
symbols.description: Builds the layers and layer_dims self-description from the shared vector cache, gated by the vectors directory mtime so repeated calls are cheap.

symbols.does: Returns the dimension of each layer.
symbols.description: Returns a mapping from layer/embedder to vector dimension, derived from the layer self-description.

symbols.does: Returns the surfaced-state lock.
symbols.description: Returns the in-process RLock that serializes read-modify-write operations on surfaced decision records across both the bridge and governance faces.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a surfaced decision.
symbols.description: Writes the surfaced decision dict to the surfaced/ directory using the durable atomic-write primitive, holding the surfaced lock for the read-modify-write.

symbols.does: Lists surfaced decisions.
symbols.description: Reads and returns all persisted surfaced decision records from the surfaced/ directory.

symbols.does: Reads a surfaced decision by id.
symbols.description: Returns the surfaced decision record for the given id, or None if it does not exist.

symbols.does: Appends an agent-mail record.
symbols.description: Writes an agent-mail record to the append-only agent mail log and returns it.

symbols.does: Queries agent mail since a sequence.
symbols.description: Returns agent-mail records with seq greater than the given value, optionally filtered by recipient, verb, thread, and limit, in oldest-first order.

symbols.does: Reads a consumer's mail cursor.
symbols.description: Returns the last consumed agent-mail sequence for the named consumer, or a default if none is recorded.

symbols.does: Records a consumer's mail cursor.
symbols.description: Atomically persists the given sequence as the last consumed position for the named consumer and returns the cursor record.
